We want the K closest points (minimum distance). Since we only care about the K smallest elements, a Max-Heap of size K is highly optimal.
Iterate over the points, calculate their distance to the origin, and push them into the Max-Heap.
If the heap size exceeds K, pop the maximum element. This guarantees the heap only holds the K smallest distances.
Since distance is x^2 + y^2, we don't even need to compute the square root to compare distances.
